title: Xtra Mild Turkish Vegetable Dumplings
description: A light and comforting Turkish dish featuring tender dumplings filled with a mix of sautéed vegetables and aromatic spices, perfect for a sick day. This vegetarian-friendly recipe is easy to make and packed with umami flavor.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and salt, then gradually add the water and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat the oil over medium heat and sauté the onion, carrots, zucchini, and mushrooms until they are tender.
Add the paprika and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Divide the dough into small pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Use a rolling pin to flatten each ball into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of the vegetable mixture in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and gently add the dumplings.
Cook the dumplings for 10-15 minutes or until they float to the surface.
